pending_context.whisper: "Bunk Beats and Mr. Farley had his radio"
pending_context.notes: Raw transcript: "We were in the ministerial allowances side of the building where they minister's stating that Bunk Beats and Mr. Farley had his radio and Mr. Jackson on the side." Context is the Vernon Dahmer firebombing aftermath (Jan 10, 1966) — speaker describing the ministerial-alliance side of the building / station where Mr. Farley (Hattiesburg minister) had his radio operation. "Bunk Beats" is a never-before-catalogued Whisper rendering. Most-likely canonical referent given context: Vernon Dahmer Sr. — though speaker may also be referring to a different Hattiesburg-area minister whose name Whisper mangled. Net-new catch; flag for adversarial.

pending_context.whisper: "I went to Mount Buebliffe or something"
pending_context.notes: Raw transcript: "I went to Mount Buebliffe or something. And come back by the Copa office." Speaker is naming a Mississippi location she visited before returning to the Hattiesburg COFO office where the "Andy the Byron" / Annie Devine secretary swap-out happened. "Mount Buebliffe" is a Whisper-mangled MS town/community name. Likely candidates: Mount Olive (Covington County MS, ~40mi NW of Hattiesburg, canonical Movement-era Black-community town), Mount Beulah (canonical CDGM Head Start training center in Edwards MS), or Mount Vernon (less likely). Mount Beulah is the canonical MFDP-organizing site context (CDGM trained there from 1965); could match. Net-new flag for adversarial.

If 96.P4.3 resolves to Mount Beulah, this is also a candidate for catalog addition — Mount Beulah was the canonical Civil Rights Movement training center on the campus of former Southern Christian Institute in Edwards MS, used by SNCC, MFDP, CDGM, and the Delta Ministry through the mid-1960s. Cross-corpus relevance: appears in Burns #70, Henderson #68, Varela #81 (CDGM cluster).

Names captured: George Harper (clear), Henry Ahring / Henry Aaron (uncertain — possibly canonical local Henry "Ahring" or homophone for "Aaron"), Stepto (canonical possibly J.C. Stepto / Joseph C. Stepto, Hattiesburg-area Movement participant), Rita (first name only — possibly Rita Schwerner Bender via the broader 1964 Freedom Summer context, OR a local Hattiesburg figure), "John and me Walker" (speaker may be referring to John Walker — Hattiesburg-area NAACP figure — or compounding two delegates). Raw transcript: "I wanted to go to all-con." Single occurrence. Speaker is reporting her sister's recall of speaker as a child wanting to go to wherever her aunt had gone before Houston, TX. Pass 3 flagged this. Pass 4 re-confirms low-confidence with three candidate readings: (a) Akron OH — canonical northern Black-migration destination from MS, plausible for a 1940s-50s aunt; (b) Auckland NZ — unlikely; (c) Alcorn State (Lorman MS) — but speaker says her aunt "ran off to" / "left to all-con" which doesn't match an in-state HBCU. Net candidate strongest = Akron OH (Hattiesburg-Akron Black-migration trail is canonical via WPA-recorded migration patterns). Retain flag for adversarial.
